THOUGHT: The miscompile occurs because InstCombine flips cttz(x, false) to cttz(x, true) when used only as a shift amount while preserving a noundef return. For x==0, cttz(x, true) can be poison, violating noundef. To preserve canonicalization and correctness, the right fix is: when performing this transform, if the return has noundef and the operand may be zero, drop the noundef attribute on the call result before setting is_zero_poison to true. This matches existing tests (they expect the flip to true without noundef) and avoids UB. I will patch InstCombineCalls.cpp accordingly, rebuild, and submit the patch.

```bash
python3 - << 'PY'
import re, subprocess, sys, pathlib

p = pathlib.Path("llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCalls.cpp")
s = p.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
orig = s

# 1) Remove any earlier guard that blocked the transform under NoUndef (if present from prior attempts)
s = s.replace(" && !II.hasRetAttr(Attribute::NoUndef)", "")

# 2) Locate the transform and modify it to drop NoUndef when Op0 may be zero.
anchor = "// If ctlz/cttz is only used as a shift amount, set is_zero_poison to true."
idx = s.find(anchor)
if idx == -1:
    print("Anchor not found; aborting.", file=sys.stderr); sys.exit(1)

# Find the return line within this transform
ret_pat = r"return\s+IC\.replaceOperand\(II,\s*1,\s*IC\.Builder\.getTrue\(\)\);\s*"
m = re.search(ret_pat, s[idx:])
if not m:
    print("Return line for setting is_zero_poison not found; aborting.", file=sys.stderr); sys.exit(1)

# Insert guard code just before the return
insert_pos = idx + m.start()
guard = """
    // If we canonicalize to is_zero_poison=true but the call has a noundef
    // return and the operand may be zero, drop noundef to avoid requiring a
    // well-defined (non-poison) result for x == 0.
    if (II.hasRetAttr(Attribute::NoUndef)) {
      auto SQ = IC.getSimplifyQuery().getWithInstruction(&II);
      if (!isKnownNonZero(Op0, SQ))
        II.removeRetAttr(Attribute::NoUndef);
    }
"""
s = s[:insert_pos] + guard + s[insert_pos:]

if s == orig:
    print("No changes made; maybe already patched.")
else:
    p.write_text(s, encoding="utf-8")
    print("Patched", p)

'''
